arts.ttl: Sources: Lopetegui on brink at West Ham, manager with 11 trophies in seven years keen to replace him
arts.sum: The Spaniard is under massive pressure...

arts.ttl: Julen Lopetegui on the brink at West Ham as Ruud van Nistelrooy kickstarts Leicester reign
arts.sum: The Hammers boss faces a nervous wait with Wolves next up on Monday

arts.ttl: Ruud van Nistelrooy enjoys winning start with Leicester
arts.sum: Leicester 3-1 West Ham: Jamie Vardy, Bilal El Khannouss and Patson Daka all scored in Van Nistelrooy’s debut

arts.ttl: Vardy starts Van Nistelrooy welcoming party as Leicester add to West Ham woe
arts.athr: Peter Lansley at the King Power Stadium
arts.sum: As one manager celebrated his first game in charge, another may have endured his last. Ruud Van Nistelrooy enjoyed a fortuitous but hard-earned victory as he could luxuriate in seeing Leicester move four points clear of the relegation zone but Julen Lopetegui must be fearing for the worst. Having spent over £100m on new players in the summer, without seeing any marked improvement in the play or results of David Moyes’s team, the former Wolves manager might be relieved to still be in post by the time his previous club visit the London Stadium on Monday. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Ruud van Nistelrooy creates Premier League history with Leicester after Man Utd exit
arts.sum: LEICESTER CITY 3-1 WEST HAM UNITED: Ruud van Nistelrooy enjoyed a stellar start to life as Foxes boss and has made Premier League history after beating them twice earlier this season while at Manchester United

arts.ttl: Leicester City 3-1 West Ham United: Highlights, man of the match, stats as Van Nistelrooy starts with emphatic victory
arts.sum: Leicester City boss Ruud van Nistelrooy got his tenure up and running with a 3-1 victory over West Ham United at the King Power Stadium.

arts.ttl: Ruud van Nistelrooy enjoys winning start with Leicester
arts.sum: Jamie Vardy, Bilal El Khannouss and Patson Daka scored in a 3-1 win over West Ham.

arts.ttl: Ruud van Nistelrooy open to Leicester City flop leaving in January
arts.sum: Ruud van Nistelrooy may need to make a big decision at Leicester City just days after his arrival at the King Power Stadium as the Foxes consider terminating Odsonne Edouard’s loan deal. The Dutch coach has replaced Steve Cooper at Leicester after the Premier League outfit experienced a slow start to the 2024/25 campaign. The Welsh […] The post Ruud van Nistelrooy open to Leicester City flop leaving in January appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Leicester City v West Ham United: Premier League – live
arts.sum: Premier League updates from the 8.15pm GMT kick-off Live scoreboard | Read Football Daily | And mail Scott The goal stands! Vardy goes racing off to celebrate, belatedly, and the King Power roars! Not a bad start to the Van Nistelrooy era, huh? 3 min: … but VAR are checking it, because Mavropanos might have been playing Vardy onside. This is going to be very tight. In fact, the naked eye suggests this goal will stand. But we await the rulers. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Leicester City team news v West Ham as Ruud van Nistelrooy names first line-up
arts.sum: City (4-2-3-1): Hermansen; Justin, Coady, Vestergaard, Kristiansen; Ndidi, Soumare; Buonanotte, El Khannouss, McAteer; Vardy. Wout Faes has been dropped for the first time this season as new manager Ruud van Nistelrooy makes three changes for his maiden Leicester City line-up.
